methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $142M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Lumafield is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $142M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine same-sector, same- or adjacent-stage companies, comprising eight private firms and one public comparable (277810.KQ). Key private peers include Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Nimble Robotics, Foundation Robotics, and Divergent, with valuations spanning $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $142M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $241M. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company universe produces $1.6B, reflecting median valuations for robotics firms at comparable or adjacent funding stages. The peer set exhibits wide dispersion, with median valuation of $1.6B against a range of $300M to $10.0B.

Synthesizing these methods yields a valuation range of $205M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $921M. The wide range reflects meaningful stage and performance dispersion within the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include Lumafield's revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and IP defensibility relative to better-capitalized peers like 1X Technologies ($10.0B). Stage-progression and peer-median methods are both applicable but produce materially different results, suggesting that company-specific operational metrics and market positioning warrant close scrutiny.
